title: Eva Unit production timeline
wikitext:
[]: Eva Unit production timeline?             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sat Sep 09, 2006 8:48 pm This has been bothering me for a while now, and I'd like some input. Simply put, when exactly were all the Evas built? I'll write out what makes the most sense to me. It's almost certainly lacking and / or grossly wrong, but bear with me. Be warned that I'm fairly new to the series and not familiar with a lot of the extra materials Gainax has released over the years, so I'm basing this primarily on what's actually in the show. Units <00: 2002-2004 The various deformed failures dumped below Central Dogma. They are the first attempts to clone Angels and produce something useful, so the construction timeline is obviously before and possibly during the development of Units 00 and 01. I'm assuming that Eva development began sometime in 2002, since that's when Gehirn and/or SEELE sent an expedition back to Antarctica in UN guise in order to see what could be retrieved, though it's possible that the project was launched earlier using genetic material that Gendo might have taken with him when he left just before Doctor Katsuragi and Adam blew everything up. On the third hand (which exists only for purposes of comparison), the failed pre-00 Units could have been based on what Gendo took when he left the Katsuragi expedition, while Unit 00 was the result of better materials salvaged two years later. Either way, the project was in an advanced stage by the time Fuyutsuki joined Gehirn. Unit 00: 2003-2004 Unit 00 seems to be the first Eva which Gehirn was actually able to use, though it remains very much the series' problem child. It was presumably begun before Unit 01, hence its designation, though the two Units' differing origins mean that they could have been built semi-concurrently. Unit 00 was in a very early stage of growth when Gendo brought Fuyutsuki to the Geofront in 2003: it consisted of little more than a head and pair of hands, all armored, and a spinal column. The head had a five-eyed design like the pre-00 Units, which was changed to the familiar cyclops version at some point later in construction. Or maybe it was actually just another pre-00, passed off as the real 00 by Gendo as an attempt to impress the good professor. Unit 01: 2003-2005 Unit 01 seems to have been only partially complete when Yui tried to synch with it in 2004, since it was still connected to Lilith but evidently had enough cyborg parts for an entry plug of some sort to be inserted. Unit 02: 2004-2006 Unit 02's construction is less thoroughly covered in the series, though it must have begun after Unit 01 and been at least semi-complete at the time of Kyoko's contact experiment in 2005. Presumably Asuka had been piloting it for a while by the time she came to Japan in 2015, but I don't think it was ever explained whether the Unit 02 that came with her is the same Unit 02 that caused Kyoko's insanity: the design could have been heavily revised in the ten years of its existence. Units 03 and 04: 2008-2010? Now for the tougher cases. Units 03 and 04 are second-generation production models, the result of Yankee Bastard Imperialistsâ„¢ wanting a piece of the action. The Eva design was changed once again after Unit 02's development, though the exact changes aren't really apparent. Since Unit 04 went 'poof' and Unit 03 got rabies, the series never explained how the pair was improved over the older models. Unit 04's much ballyhooed S2 engine wasn't an original part of the design specs, though the fact it was sent from Germany to Nevada suggests that 04 was the least glitchy of the Evas available or was NERV's guinea pig Unit at the time. Both Units were done by 2015, and probably earlier than that. A line of dialogue in the beginning of episode 17 mentions synchro data from Unit 04 being available, though who or what Second Branch was using to test its Evas was never explained. I think the construction period for these Units best fits midway between Unit 02 and Units 05-13, since I don't see much sense in NERV building Unit 02 and then suddenly waiting ten years to start the next batch. (As a fun but irrelevant side note, Shinji's pallet magnum from episode 16 is probably also a Second Branch product, as the original Desert Eagle design is American and the pistol was being made in the US when NGE was produced. Assuming that Israel flooded after Second Impact, it would still be made in the US in 2015.) Units 05 through 13: 2014-2015 The third-generation production model Evas are more or less MacGuffined into continuity during the End of Everything. Hyuga tells Misato that mass production had just begun at the time of episode 22, but I suspect that it had been going on as a camouflaged operation before that. Units 05 and 06 must have been known to NERV's Japanese branch already, as spare parts from them were being shipped to Japan for use on Unit 00, but they certainly weren't supposed to be done in time for the events of EoE. Units XX, XY and XZ: 2012-2015? As for the three Simulation Bodies attacked by the 12th Angel, I honestly have no idea. The best I can come up with is that they're fairly new, as the section of NERV HQ that houses them is mentioned as being of recent construction. Any suggestions? Well, that's everything I can remember and find from watching episodes 17 through 22'. If I've totally fumbled, hopefully one of ye olde veterans will straighten me out. Keep the flames below 200Â°, please. Apologies if this has been done before; I did a forum search but didn't find anything. So how long does it actually take to build an Eva? I assume that the process was streamlined as Gehirn / NERV progressed, but it must have taken years in the beginning and months by the time the MP Evas were made. There was something else I wanted to add, but now I can't remember what it was. I'll post it when it comes back to me.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Sep 10, 2006 4:25 am The "missing decade" 2005-2014 is one of the weakest bits of the plot; needed to give the main characters traumata in infancy and be teens when the action comes to a head. The stretch between the contact experiments and the commissioning dates (which for EVA-00 is 2014, and of 01, 2015, according to the data in the OP) is odd. There may be a thread elsewhere worrying about how long does it take to gestate something the size of a full grown blue whale anyway.

[]: Re: Eva Unit production timeline?             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Sep 10, 2006 11:49 am BobBQ wrote: The various deformed failures dumped below Central Dogma.

[]: Re: Eva Unit production timeline?             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sun Sep 10, 2006 3:44 pm Reichu wrote: A lot of them aren't really "deformed" -- just much of their skeletons are missing.

[]: Re: Eva Unit production timeline?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Sep 10, 2006 4:41 pm BobBQ wrote: There was a scene--which I for some reason can't find right now--which showed the skeleton of a pre-00 Eva laid out; its lower structure looked pretty vestigal to me.

[]:             Quote Post by Rawinder » Sun Sep 10, 2006 4:49 pm I don't really understand the how and when of Eva-02's birth. It must've been in 2004-ish for Kyoko's CE to go horribly wrong. But didn't Asuka herself say that Eva-02 was the first Eva built to combat Angels (the first of which didn't appear until over a decade later)?

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sun Sep 10, 2006 5:16 pm Rawinder wrote: I don't really understand the how and when of Eva-02's birth. It must've been in 2004-ish for Kyoko's CE to go horribly wrong. But didn't Asuka herself say that Eva-02 was the first Eva built to combat Angels (the first of which didn't appear until over a decade later)?

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Mon Sep 11, 2006 12:16 am BobBQ wrote: Pretty badly deformed, if you ask me.
